findings.location: Independent review (before the author sees the design — L3)
findings.issue: The section defining this skill's own reviewer (the fresh-context check of architecture.md) lists four checklist bullets (gate spec complete, dependency model coherent, contested choices threaded, gate load within budget) but never defines a scored rubric — no dimensions summing to 100, no /85 threshold, no critical-flags list, and no named rubric file. This is exactly the structure L3 and skill_quality_rubric.md require of every other content-generating step's reviewer ('Scored rubrics: dimensions sum to 100, threshold 85, plus binary critical flags that block regardless of score'), and which page-architecture itself mandates (step 3, 'Drafter/reviewer pairings') for every stage of the plugin under design. The skill that writes the rule for everyone else does not visibly follow it for its own gate.
findings.fix: Either point to an explicit architecture-review rubric file (dimensions/100, threshold 85, critical flags, output schema — mirroring skill_quality_rubric.md's own structure) or inline that schema directly in this section, so architecture_review.json has a score/threshold/critical_flags shape instead of an unscored checklist.
findings.resolution: Inspection-vs-scored-rubric justification (Fagan/IEEE 1028) stated in the Independent review section (1.1)

findings.issue: Step 9 is a single sentence pointing to gate_design_patterns.md's 'Session mechanics' and naming a few component categories (agents, gate app, scripts, reference files), with no concrete guidance on what the inventory output itself should record per component beyond 'decide what is generated by page-scaffold vs. authored by page-draft.'
findings.fix: Add one or two lines on what the component-inventory output should capture per item (name, type, generated-by, consumed-by) so a cold agent isn't left inferring the shape from a cross-reference alone.
findings.resolution: Component-inventory schema left as cross-reference; revisit if a pilot shows divergence

findings.location: Output — architecture.md, Data-flow & security model bullet
findings.issue: The output section allows an explicit 'not applicable' escape hatch for plugins touching no student data/external services/generated UI, but never states who confirms that determination — the drafting step could assert N/A unilaterally without author sign-off, which is exactly the kind of judgment call the gate exists to surface.
findings.fix: Add a clause that the 'not applicable' determination on the data-flow/security model is itself one of the items reviewed at architecture_gate, not something the drafting step may assert unilaterally.
findings.resolution: The n/a escape hatch is part of the gated architecture.md the author approves; explicit per-gate confirmation deferred

resolution_pass.legend: status=fixed: addressed in the named release; status=accepted: deliberate, documented deviation or deferred minor
